question: First identify the state that appears in both of these data.gov-derived groups: Which states fall 4th through 8th when you look for where diabetes-care copays varied the most from one 2024 health insurance plan to another? Break any ties alphabetically.; and Which states fall 6th through 10th when you compare the typical electricity prices businesses paid in different ZIP codes served by investor-owned utilities in 2016? Break any ties alphabetically.. After identifying that state, where did the first settlement of non-indigenous people there occur in 1827? Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/plan-attributes-puf-py2024/files/plan_attributes_PUF.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

state_map = {
    "AL": "Alabama", "AK": "Alaska", "AZ": "Arizona", "AR": "Arkansas", "CA": "California",
    "CO": "Colorado", "CT": "Connecticut", "DE": "Delaware", "FL": "Florida", "GA": "Georgia",
    "HI": "Hawaii", "ID": "Idaho", "IL": "Illinois", "IN": "Indiana", "IA": "Iowa",
    "KS": "Kansas", "KY": "Kentucky", "LA": "Louisiana", "ME": "Maine", "MD": "Maryland",
    "MA": "Massachusetts", "MI": "Michigan", "MN": "Minnesota", "MS": "Mississippi", "MO": "Missouri",
    "MT": "Montana", "NE": "Nebraska", "NV": "Nevada", "NH": "New Hampshire", "NJ": "New Jersey",
    "NM": "New Mexico", "NY": "New York", "NC": "North Carolina", "ND": "North Dakota", "OH": "Ohio",
    "OK": "Oklahoma", "OR": "Oregon", "PA": "Pennsylvania", "RI": "Rhode Island", "SC": "South Carolina",
    "SD": "South Dakota", "TN": "Tennessee", "TX": "Texas", "UT": "Utah", "VT": "Vermont",
    "VA": "Virginia", "WA": "Washington", "WV": "West Virginia", "WI": "Wisconsin", "WY": "Wyoming",
    "DC": "District of Columbia"
}

df["state"] = df["StateCode"].map(state_map)
df["copayment_amount"] = pd.to_numeric(
    df["SBCHavingDiabetesCopayment"].astype(str).str.replace(r"[^0-9.-]", "", regex=True),
    errors="coerce"
)

state_metric = (
    df.dropna(subset=["state", "copayment_amount"])
      .groupby("state", as_index=False)["copayment_amount"]
      .std()
      .rename(columns={"copayment_amount": "stddev_copayment_amount"})
)

ranked = state_metric.sort_values(["stddev_copayment_amount", "state"], ascending=[False, True]).reset_index(drop=True)
answer = ranked.iloc[3:8]["state"].tolist()
1.subquestion: Which states fall 4th through 8th when you look for where diabetes-care copays varied the most from one 2024 health insurance plan to another? Break any ties alphabetically.

2.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/u-s-electric-utility-companies-and-rates-look-up-by-zipcode-2016-87bcc/files/iouzipcodes2016.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

state_map = {
    "AL": "Alabama", "AK": "Alaska", "AZ": "Arizona", "AR": "Arkansas", "CA": "California",
    "CO": "Colorado", "CT": "Connecticut", "DE": "Delaware", "FL": "Florida", "GA": "Georgia",
    "HI": "Hawaii", "ID": "Idaho", "IL": "Illinois", "IN": "Indiana", "IA": "Iowa",
    "KS": "Kansas", "KY": "Kentucky", "LA": "Louisiana", "ME": "Maine", "MD": "Maryland",
    "MA": "Massachusetts", "MI": "Michigan", "MN": "Minnesota", "MS": "Mississippi", "MO": "Missouri",
    "MT": "Montana", "NE": "Nebraska", "NV": "Nevada", "NH": "New Hampshire", "NJ": "New Jersey",
    "NM": "New Mexico", "NY": "New York", "NC": "North Carolina", "ND": "North Dakota", "OH": "Ohio",
    "OK": "Oklahoma", "OR": "Oregon", "PA": "Pennsylvania", "RI": "Rhode Island", "SC": "South Carolina",
    "SD": "South Dakota", "TN": "Tennessee", "TX": "Texas", "UT": "Utah", "VT": "Vermont",
    "VA": "Virginia", "WA": "Washington", "WV": "West Virginia", "WI": "Wisconsin", "WY": "Wyoming",
    "DC": "District of Columbia"
}

df["state_full"] = df["state"].map(state_map)
df["ind_rate_num"] = pd.to_numeric(df["ind_rate"], errors="coerce")

state_metric = (
    df.dropna(subset=["state_full", "ind_rate_num"])
      .groupby("state_full", as_index=False)["ind_rate_num"]
      .median()
      .rename(columns={"ind_rate_num": "median_ind_rate"})
)

ranked = state_metric.sort_values(["median_ind_rate", "state_full"], ascending=[False, True]).reset_index(drop=True)
answer = ranked.iloc[5:10]["state_full"].tolist()

Hop result: semantic intersection of <node 1 answer> and <node 2 answer> = Kansas.
